DATA ACCESS 
 
After registration, you will be able to download our data. To assist you, our 
HelpCenter is available, and more specifically its section about download. 
 
Information on operational issues on products and services can be found on 
our User Notification Service. If needed, you can contact us . 
 
I INTRODUCTION 
I.1. Summary 
This document is the user manual for the Copernicus Marine Service Ocean Monitoring 
Indicator (OMI) OMI_CLIMATE_OSC_MEDSEA_volume_mean. It provides a time series of Ocean 
Salt Content (OSC) volume averaged over the Mediterran ean Sea (30°N, 46°N; 6°W, 36°E) , 
between 0 and 300 meter s depth, excluding regions where the seafloor is shallower than 300 
meters (see the QUID document for more details on the calculation method). 
The product consists of a one-dimensional (time) dataset with annual mean of OSC, computed 
from a combination of reanalysis and observational products: MEDREA1, CGLOR, ORAS, FOAM 
reanalysis ( from the GREP2 ensemble), and CORA5.2 3 and ARMOR3D4 observational salinity 
data. 
The time series begins in 1993 and, for each product, all available data is used, resulting in a 
varying time coverage. Consequently,

catalog_entry.summary: This document is a Product User Manual for OMI_CLIMATE_OSC_MEDSEA, providing details on Ocean Salinity Content volume averaged over the Mediterranean Sea from 1993 onwards using various reanalysis and observational datasets.

variables.long_name: Volume averaged Ocean Salinity Content (0-300m) in the Mediterranean Sea
